name: Los Calderones de Piedrasecha
region_intro: Hiking around Carrocera offers diverse natural landscapes near the Cantabrian Mountains, characterized by gorges, ancient beech forests, and mountainous terrain. The region features limestone bedrock, carved by rivers, creating unique geological formations within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. This area provides a captivating landscape for outdoor enthusiasts. Trails cater to various skill levels, from gentle forest walks to more challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Carrocera The most popular hiking route is Los Calderones de Piedrasecha, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This easy path traverses a dramatic gorge where the stream sometimes disappears underground. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Bustillo Loop from Los Barrios de Gordón, a moderate 7.7 miles (12.4 km) path. This route leads through mountainous terrain within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. Local hikers also love the Cruz de Fontañán – Pico Fontañán Summit (1,634 m) loop from Olleros de Alba, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Carrocera is defined by dramatic gorges, ancient beech forests, and diverse mountain landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Carrocera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Carrocera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Carrocera?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails in the Carrocera area, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy strolls, moderate paths, and some more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of landscapes and terrain can I expect on hikes near Carrocera?
A: Hiking around Carrocera offers diverse natural landscapes, including dramatic gorges carved by rivers, ancient beech forests, and mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by limestone bedrock, creating unique geological formations and impressive views, especially within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Carrocera offers several easy routes. A great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041085482/los-calderones-de-piedrasecha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones de Piedrasecha&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.7-mile (6.0 km) trail that takes less than two hours to complete. This path traverses a scenic gorge where the stream sometimes disappears underground, offering a unique experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several circular routes allow you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041052597/hayedo-la-boyariza-circular-from-geras-de-gordon-reserva-de-la-biosfera-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hayedo La Boyariza – circular from Geras de Gordón&lt;/a&gt;, which explores ancient beech forests within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Fontañán – Pico Fontañán Summit (1,634 m) loop from La Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The region boasts several notable natural features. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or discover mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fontañán Summit (1,634 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Bustillo (Peña la Muezca)&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Carrocera?
A: The trails in Carrocera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic gorges, the tranquility of the ancient beech forests, and the diverse mountain landscapes that offer options for all ability levels.

Q: Is Carrocera a good destination for hiking with dogs?
A: While specific dog-friendly regulations can vary by trail and protected area, the diverse natural landscapes and numerous trails around Carrocera generally offer good opportunities for hiking with dogs. Always ensure your dog is on a leash and check local signage for any restrictions, especially in nature reserves.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking more challenging ascents, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051293060/pena-la-muezca-or-pico-bustillo-1-859-m-from-los-barrios-de-gordon-alto-bernesga-biosphere-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña la Muezca or Pico Bustillo (1.859 m) from Los Barrios de Gordón&lt;/a&gt; offer a rewarding experience. This 7.7-mile (12.4 km) trail leads through mountainous terrain and typically takes over 4 hours to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Carrocera?
A: The region's diverse landscapes, including forests and mountains, make it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with autumn being particularly beautiful in the beech forests. Summer can be warm, but higher altitude trails provide cooler options. Winter hiking is possible, but some routes may require appropriate gear due to snow or ice.

Q: Are there any gorges or canyons to explore in the Carrocera area?
A: Yes, the Carrocera region is known for its impressive gorges and canyons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering dramatic scenery where the stream sometimes flows underground. The broader region of León, where Carrocera is located, is famous for such geological formations, often featuring unique rock formations and clear waters. For more information on gorges and walkways in Spain, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.spain.info/en/top/gorges-tourist-walkways-spain/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;spain.info&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads near Carrocera?
A: While Carrocera is a rural area, some larger towns nearby may have limited public transport connections. For specific trailheads, it's often advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving, as many routes might be more easily accessible by car. Parking information is usually available on individual route pages.
tips: The Virgen del Monedero hermitage, which we access by stairs, is located inside the Cueva de las Palomas. A fairytale place that is definitely worth visiting.
A beautiful waterfall that we found walking next to the Calderones stream, very close to the Santas Martas cabin. Following the Calderones gorge, we will find more waterfalls.

Popular place for hiking, especially near the waterfall of the Calderones stream. The cabin offers a picturesque view of what was once an old cattle village, now in ruins, with only one building standing.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves
Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones
